pattern: A run of one repeated letter is the only place a same-letter block can come from, so per letter, only its three longest runs can ever combine to produce three occurrences of some length.
hints: First split the string into maximal runs of one repeated letter (e.g. 'aaabaaaa' becomes runs of a, b, a with certain lengths) and look at each letter's runs separately from every other letter's.
For one letter, think about the three ways to get three occurrences of a length-k block: all three from one run, two from the longest run and one from the next, or one each from three different runs.

So for each letter keep only its top three run lengths r1 >= r2 >= r3, and the best length for that letter is the largest of (r1 - 2), (the smaller of r1 - 1 and r2), and r3 -- then take the max over every letter, or -1 if nothing qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a single run long enough to repeat a pair three times
statement example, no letter ever repeats a block
statement example, only single letters repeat three times
minimum length, one run exactly long enough for length 1
a letter appearing only as three separate single-character runs
requires combining the two longest runs of the same letter, not just cubing the longest one
one long single run of the same letter

stress.description: short random strings over a tiny alphabet so repeats are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 50))
    alphabet = 'abc'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
